evals.prompt: I need to add rate limiting to our API. We're getting hammered by brute force attempts and need to throttle requests per IP.
evals.expected_output: A plan with tasks covering: middleware implementation, config for rate limits, storage backend (redis/memory), tests, and documentation. Should produce a plan file and offer to create issues.
expectations: Output contains a task breakdown table with columns: ID, Title, Type, Priority, Effort, Deps
Has a parent task (T1) representing the overall goal
Has subtasks (T1.1, T1.2, etc.) under the parent task
Parent task includes a Current Flow section
Parent task includes a New Flow (after implementation) section
Includes a test subtask as the last subtask
Each subtask has Acceptance Criteria
Asks user to review/approve the plan before creating files
Offers to create issues on GitHub or Jira

evals.prompt: /planner Add request/response logging with body dump capability — we need to be able to debug failures in production
evals.expected_output: A plan that analyzes the existing codebase, breaks down logging implementation into tasks, considers sensitive data masking, and creates a structured plan file.
expectations: Output contains a task breakdown table with columns: ID, Title, Type, Priority, Effort, Deps
Has a parent task (T1) with Current Flow and New Flow sections
Includes subtasks covering logging implementation
Considers sensitive data masking or redaction in the plan
Includes a test subtask as the last subtask
Each subtask lists Affected Files
Offers to save plan to docs/plans/ directory

evals.prompt: /planner Refactor the validation layer to support multiple token types (JWT, API key, OAuth2) instead of just the current single validation approach
evals.expected_output: A refactoring plan with strategy pattern or similar, phased approach to avoid breaking changes, migration tasks, and comprehensive test updates.
expectations: Output contains a task breakdown table
Parent task includes Current Flow showing single validation approach
Parent task includes New Flow showing multi-token support
Subtasks are ordered by dependency to avoid breaking changes
Mentions a design pattern (strategy, adapter, or similar)
Includes a test subtask covering all token types
Subtask types include 'refactor' for at least one task
Each subtask has Acceptance Criteria including no behavior change for existing tests

evals.prompt: /planner We have a monorepo with packages/api, packages/web, and packages/shared. Add user profile editing with avatar upload.
evals.expected_output: A plan that detects the monorepo structure and uses [service]: prefix in subtask titles (e.g., [api]: Add avatar upload endpoint, [web]: Add profile edit form, [shared]: Add validation schema). Should group work by service.
expectations: Detects or acknowledges the monorepo structure
Subtask titles use [service-name]: prefix format (e.g., [api]:, [web]:, [shared]:)
Has subtasks spanning multiple services/packages
Parent task includes Current Flow and New Flow sections
Includes a test subtask as the last subtask
Each subtask lists Affected Files with paths relative to the service
Offers to create issues on GitHub or Jira

evals.prompt: /planner Add dark mode support to the app
evals.expected_output: A plan for a single-repo project that uses plain subtask titles without [service]: prefix. Should cover theme system, CSS variables, toggle component, persistence, and tests.
expectations: Subtask titles do NOT use [service]: prefix format
Uses plain descriptive titles for subtasks
Parent task includes Current Flow and New Flow sections
Includes a test subtask as the last subtask
Covers theme system or CSS variables in at least one subtask
Covers a toggle component or user preference persistence
Each subtask has Acceptance Criteria
